purpose: Define evidence quality tiers for research and validation review.

tiers.label: Primary verified evidence
tiers.description: Direct official or source-level evidence such as company filings, official guidance, central bank statements, government data, or confirmed exchange disclosures.
tiers.usage: Can anchor case validation when timestamp and context are clear.

tiers.label: Verified secondary evidence
tiers.description: Reliable reporting, analyst transcripts, reputable data vendors, or cross-checked market summaries.
tiers.usage: Can support interpretation but should be checked against primary evidence when possible.

tiers.label: Market signal evidence
tiers.description: Price action, volume, breadth, volatility, leader reaction, rates, currency, or cross-asset confirmation.
tiers.usage: Useful for reaction validation but must be interpreted with timing and context.

tiers.label: Narrative and attention evidence
tiers.description: Market commentary, social attention, repeated storylines, media framing, or AI-amplified summaries.
tiers.usage: Useful for Narrative Intelligence but not sufficient alone for production scoring.

tiers.label: Unverified evidence
tiers.description: Rumors, uncited claims, single-source speculation, or incomplete reports.
tiers.usage: Can be logged as context but must not drive decisions without confirmation.

rules: Evidence tier is for validation and review only.
Evidence tier must not directly change Theme Score, Stage Score, or Decision Score.
Lower-tier evidence requires higher confirmation before use in lessons or playbooks.
Single-source unverified evidence must not create a Buy decision.
